LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE RE: PRETEXT BASED ON EVIDENCE PLAINTIFF HAS SUBMITTED THAT THE CITY DID NOT REQUIRE HER A GRADE 3 LICENSE ON THE TIME FRAME PRESENTED. THE COURT ALSO ALLOWS A 2 HOUR DEPO RE: DOCUMENTS THAT MS. HOGAN HAS PUT FORTH TODAY IN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO THE EXTENT THAT THE CITY HAS NOT PREVIOUSLY DONE SO. THE CITY CAN ALSO FILE ANOTHER SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION IF DEFENDANT CAN ESTABLISH GOOD CAUSE WHY THERE ARE DIFFERENT FACTORS AND CIRCUMSTANCES. IN ADDITION, IF PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON DISCOVERY, PARTIES MAY FILE A MOTION. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 437C(G) AND COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Ruling Denying Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Adrienne Hogan, Telephone 925-204-5562, appearing on her own behalf as Pro Per Plaintiff;
Amy D Super, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, City and County of San Franicso, Telephone 415-554-3931, appearing for Defendant CCSF;

Ms. Hogan is administered the Oath under penalty of perjury at the start of todays hearing. Court inquires of Ms. Hogan if in 2002 she held the position of shift operator. Under oath Ms Hogan states that she was not a shift operator in 2002.

Ruing - A R G U E D: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Reconsideration Of Ruling Denying Motion For Summary Judgment. (specially set at 9:00 am before Judge Kahn)

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for reconsideration of the January 9, 2019 order denying the City's motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable dispute whether plaintiff Adrienne Hogan was a "shift operator" as that term is used in 22 CCR 63770. Ms. Hogan says she wasn't and the City says she was. While Ms. Hogan's statements that she was not a shift operator were not explicitly made in a declaration under penalty of perjury, that omission may be cured by administering the oath to her at the hearing and is not a basis for a non-legally trained self-represented litigant to lose her claims.

Plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order approved as to form and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge:Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com =(302/HEK)

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication.

The Court also allows a 2 hour Depo re: documents that Ms. Hogan has put forth today in opposition to the Motion to the extent that the City has not previously done so. The City can also file another Summary Judgment motion if Defendant can establish good cause why there are different factors and circumstances. In addition, if parties cannot agree on Discovery, parties may file a motion.

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CCP 437c(g) and compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Judgment For Deft And Order Granting Deft'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jul-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Adrienne Hogan, 925-204-5562 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Kate Kimberlin, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3800 for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant Argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ADRIENNE HOGAN's Motion To Set Aside Judgment For Defendant And Order Granting Defendant's Motion For Summary Judgment.

The Court adopts the tenatative ruling with modification on the hearing date and briefing schedule. Plaintiff Adrienne Hogan's motion to set aside the judgment and order granting defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is granted. Ms. Hogan has shown that she is entitled to discretionary relief per CCP 437(b) because her mistaken belief that the November 2, 2017 order continuing the trial date also continued the date of her opposition to the City's motion for summary judgment was made in good faith and was reasonable in light of the relevant circumstances. Prior to substituting out of the case, Ms. Hogan's former counsel, Mr. Patten, informed Ms. Hogan that the stipulated motion seeking continuance would result in a new date for Ms. Hogan to respond to the motion for summary judgment. The November 21 order also stated that pretrial deadlines will be recalculated according to the new trial date. As a self-represented litigant, Ms. Hogan was reasonably diligent in bringing this motion, which was filed within six months of the entry of judgment on December 19, 2017. Ms. Hogan may file opposition papers to the City's motion for summary judgment no later than Septemebr 5, 2018. The City may file reply papers no later than Septemeber 18, 2018. The motion will be heard on Septemebr 26, 2018. Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and the City has not identified any legal prejudice it will suffer if Ms. Hogan is given another opportunity to oppose the City's motion for summary judgment.

Plaintiff, Ms. Hoganto submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
